title: Implement Ops read model and freshness substrate
next_action: Define/read normalized Ops records from existing ledgers with freshness values before adding command mutations.
acceptance: Ops read model can distinguish live, idle, stale, parked, and archived records from plan/task/run/proof/job sources without showing stale debt as live work.

title: Define Ops command and stream boundaries after read model
next_action: After read/freshness proof, define command endpoint and /ws/ops stream boundaries for approve, park, cancel, promote, archive, and purge operations.
acceptance: Command/stream boundaries are documented with hard stops for purge/destructive behavior and CLI-equivalent operations.

title: Build Ops Jobs tab MVP shell
next_action: Build the Workbench Ops Jobs tab shell with six lanes, Stale Debt drawer, inspector, and record-type visual tokens after read/freshness data is available.
acceptance: Jobs tab renders from Ops read data, quarantines stale debt, exposes inspector actions as disabled or preview-only until command endpoints exist, and passes Studio browser proof.

title: Wire live platform uploads for the autopilot faceless business
next_action: Implement official API upload paths for YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram so live=true plus credentials flips staged bundles into real uploads with permalink logging.
acceptance: With credentials and live=true, a rendered video uploads through official APIs, retries/rate limits are handled, and a permalink is recorded.
blockers: Platform credentials and live publishing approval are required before real upload proof can run.

title: Replace simulated metrics with real analytics ingestion for the autopilot faceless business
next_action: Pull real platform performance metrics into the feedback loop so concept selection uses observed views/watch/CTR/revenue instead of simulated rows.
acceptance: Performance rows ingest real platform metrics with simulated:false and update niche weights from observed outcomes.
blockers: Platform analytics credentials and live platform accounts are required before real-ingest proof can run.

title: Add AI b-roll and thumbnail generation to the autopilot faceless business
next_action: Integrate budget-aware b-roll and thumbnail generation so scenes can source visuals within the daily spend cap.
acceptance: Per-scene visuals are sourced or generated within budget and a thumbnail artifact is rendered for publish-ready bundles.

title: Add channel-growth tracking for build-and-flip candidates
next_action: Track channel growth against sale thresholds and flag sale-ready candidates in the autopilot lane.
acceptance: Channel growth is tracked toward sale thresholds and sale-ready channels are flagged with evidence-backed thresholds.

title: Enable live client offer email sending for the autopilot faceless business
next_action: Wire Resend or SMTP sending so staged client-upsell offers can actually send when sender details, provider credentials, and live=true are present.
acceptance: Offer emails send successfully with provider credentials, sender identity, and bounce/reply handling.
blockers: Provider credentials and sender configuration are required before live email proof can run.

title: Harden the autopilot faceless production run loop
next_action: Install cron-driven ticks, circuit breakers, a simple dashboard, and post-eval integration so the lane can run unattended with failure visibility.
acceptance: Scheduled ticks run in production, consecutive failures trip a breaker, and status/dashboard proof exists.

title: Create the monetization-account handoff packet for the autonomous content engine
next_action: Create the required affiliate/ad-network accounts and record the chosen provider/account state so the engine can legally monetize.
acceptance: Amazon Associates and/or ad-network account setup state is captured and the engine has the required publisher identifiers without committing secrets.
blockers: Affiliate/ad-network accounts must be created by Fritz; an agent cannot legally sign up as him.

title: Package the autonomous content engine deploy and publish path
next_action: Add the repo-safe deploy/publish path so the cron-driven content engine can build and ship the static site from the canonical runtime lane.
acceptance: A documented command path generates the site, publishes it to the chosen host, and leaves proof artifacts for the last run.

title: Add quality and ranking hardening for the autonomous content engine
next_action: Strengthen quality gates, disclosure, and anti-spam safeguards so the engine stays asset-grade rather than scaled-content sludge.
acceptance: The generation path enforces quality/disclosure gates and the operator docs clearly state the anti-spam constraints and review points.

title: Bind the autonomous content engine to a live domain and monetization-ready runtime
next_action: Point a domain at the deploy target and wire the chosen monetization identifiers so the engine runs on the intended public property.
acceptance: The site is reachable on the chosen domain and monetization/provider identifiers are configured without leaking secrets.
blockers: Domain assignment and publisher-account details require Fritz-owned access and decisions.

title: Build the market-data ingest lane for financial agents
next_action: Create the daily market snapshot ingest path so each strategy agent starts from the same recent market data.
acceptance: A repeatable daily market snapshot is captured from the chosen free/keyless source and stored for strategy evaluation.

title: Implement the first strategy fleet and $10 leaderboard for financial agents
next_action: Define the initial strategy-agent set and leaderboard calculation so each session produces comparable paper-trading outcomes.
acceptance: Multiple strategy agents run on the same market snapshot and emit a leaderboard showing what each $10 position became at the close.

title: Add evaluation and reporting for the financial-agents lane
next_action: Record multi-session performance and expose the leaderboard in a reusable report/briefing surface instead of one-off output.
acceptance: Performance is tracked across sessions and surfaced in a stable report that distinguishes smoke-test results from longer-horizon signal.

title: Prepare the broker-gated paper/live execution adapter for financial agents
next_action: Design the paper/live brokerage adapter behind a hard gate so later execution can route through broker credentials without changing the evaluation harness.
acceptance: A broker adapter plan exists with clear paper-first gating and no live execution occurs without broker credentials and explicit approval.
blockers: Broker account choice and API credentials are required before any paper/live brokerage proof can run.

title: Choose the client stack and execution lane for the Fritz Companion App
next_action: Pick the thin-client direction (PWA vs React Native vs reuse of an existing shell) so the app build lane can start without stack drift.
acceptance: One client-stack decision is recorded with rationale, canonical repo path, and follow-on implementation expectations.

title: Define secure off-LAN access for the Fritz Companion App
next_action: Bind the app to a secure auth/transport path for reaching the Shay gateway when Fritz is off-LAN.
acceptance: A remote-access path is selected and documented with API_SERVER_KEY or equivalent auth gating before any phone client exposure.
blockers: Remote gateway exposure and auth policy are not yet built and require an explicit security decision.

title: Build the thin mobile chat and briefing shell for the Fritz Companion App
next_action: Implement the first mobile-first chat client that surfaces the Command Center briefing and routes actions through the Shay gateway.
acceptance: A mobile-first client can load the daily briefing, send a prompt, receive a response, and preserve the second-in-command interaction model.

title: Define proactive-send policy and push handoff for the Fritz Companion App
next_action: Specify what Shay may proactively send versus what should wait for Fritz, and tie that policy to the future push channel.
acceptance: A clear proactive-send policy exists and maps the app to the shared reach/push fabric without spamming Fritz.

title: Add a canonical build/refresh command for Mission Control / Command Center
next_action: Expose the command-center generator behind a stable command path so the dashboard and briefing can be rebuilt on demand or by cron.
acceptance: One documented command regenerates command-center/index.html, briefing.md, and state.json from the live ledgers.

title: Mount Mission Control / Command Center into Studio and the Workbench/Shay Desk surfaces
next_action: Serve the generated dashboard through the existing Studio/Ops/Shay Desk surfaces so it is not just a standalone artifact.
acceptance: The dashboard is reachable from the intended product surfaces and remains read-only against the underlying ledgers.

title: Deliver the morning Mission Control briefing to Fritz's phone automatically
next_action: Connect the command-center briefing output to the future phone/reach surface so Fritz receives the morning rundown without pulling it manually.
acceptance: The daily briefing lands automatically on Fritz's phone through an approved delivery path and references the live command-center state.
blockers: Automatic phone delivery depends on the reach/push fabric and approved channel choices.

title: Promote billing capability drafts into send-capable Mission Control actions
next_action: Choose the payment/provider path and wire billing capability drafts into real, gated sending behavior.
acceptance: Billing actions can generate and send through an approved provider path with proper credentials and proof logging.
blockers: Payment-provider choice and credentials are required before send-capable billing proof can run.

title: Promote work capability drafts into real Jira/Slack-connected Mission Control actions
next_action: Bind the work-draft capabilities to the chosen Jira/Slack surfaces once credentials and scope are approved.
acceptance: Work actions can draft and deliver through approved Jira/Slack connections with proof-backed logs.
blockers: Jira/Slack credentials and approved scopes are required before live delivery proof can run.

title: Finish the landscape report for Shay as an omnipresent virtual assistant
next_action: Complete the build-on survey of existing products, open-source building blocks, reach fabric options, and virtual-body candidates.
acceptance: The landscape report names the strongest stack recommendation and the major lock-ins/cost tradeoffs by channel.

title: Define the reach fabric for Shay's omnipresent assistant layer
next_action: Choose the shared channel-delivery architecture that can reach push/SMS/voice/Telegram/email and fall back cleanly.
acceptance: One reach-fabric design exists with fallback rules, delivery priorities, and integration expectations for phone/web/voice surfaces.

title: Run the virtual-body tradeoff study for Shay's omnipresent assistant layer
next_action: Compare avatar/voice/persona options for always-on use, focusing on cost, latency, and believable presence.
acceptance: A tradeoff study exists that recommends a virtual-body path or explicitly parks it with cost/latency evidence.

title: Map the wearable and smart-glasses ubiquity lane for Shay
next_action: Characterize the platform limits and best available delivery patterns for watch, voice-assistant, and smart-glasses surfaces.
acceptance: A platform-constraint map exists for the wearable/glasses lane with clear build-now vs blocked-by-platform boundaries.
